top_stories.title: Cisco Adds NHI to Security Stack With Astrix, WideField Acquisitions
top_stories.summary: Cisco has acquired Astrix and WideField to add non-human identity (NHI) security capabilities to its security stack. The acquisitions position Cisco to manage and secure machine identities, service accounts, and API credentials as part of a broader identity-as-control-plane strategy.

top_stories.title: Robinhood Cuts Access Approval Time to Support High-Velocity Development
top_stories.summary: Robinhood's engineering security team overhauled its system access approval process to reduce approval time while maintaining security controls for developers. The initiative focused on streamlining access management workflows to support faster development cycles in a fintech environment.

top_stories.title: Scope of Salesforce Attacks Expands as Icarus Leaks Data
top_stories.summary: Attackers breached application vendor Klue and leveraged its OAuth tokens to access and steal Salesforce customer data, with additional victims now being identified. The attack vector centers on compromised OAuth tokens, highlighting risks in third-party application authorization chains.

top_stories.title: He Thought He Was Secure; His Phone Number Was Stolen Anyway
top_stories.summary: The article covers SIM swap attacks as a method for stealing SMS-based one-time passwords, leading to account takeovers. The piece highlights the vulnerability of SMS-based MFA and the need for stronger authentication methods.

top_stories.title: Proof’s x401 establishes an open protocol for AI agent identity and authorization
top_stories.summary: Proof has launched x401, an open issuer-neutral protocol designed to handle identity verification and authorization for AI agents interacting with websites and APIs. The protocol allows services to request and verify credentials covering identity, organizational affiliation, signing authority, proof of humanness, and other claims before permitting agent actions.

news.title: Mirage2FA phishing kit uses HTML smuggling to steal Microsoft 365 credentials
news.summary: Researchers at Fortra identified the Mirage2FA phishing kit, which uses HTML smuggling and obfuscated JavaScript to deliver fake Microsoft 365 login pages that steal credentials during MFA prompts. The campaign uses business-themed lures such as secure documents, remittance services, and payment requests to trick users into entering credentials on fraudulent pages.

news.title: SIM-swapping gang busted in international police operation
news.summary: Polish authorities arrested four members of a cybercrime group conducting SIM swap attacks, cryptocurrency theft, and money laundering in an operation coordinated with the FBI and HSI. SIM swapping is a method used to bypass SMS-based MFA by hijacking victims' phone numbers to intercept authentication codes.

news.title: Amazon Q Developer Flaw Could Let Malicious Repos Run Code via MCP Configs
news.summary: A high-severity vulnerability (CVE-2026-12957, CVSS 8.5) in Amazon Q Developer allowed malicious repositories to execute commands and steal cloud credentials through mishandled Model Context Protocol (MCP) server configurations. Amazon has patched the flaw, which required a developer to open and trust a malicious repository for the exploit to trigger.

news.title: Guardian Agents: The Next Layer of Identity Governance
news.summary: The article examines how AI agents operating in enterprise environments are creating governance gaps in existing identity infrastructure built for human access. The piece addresses the challenge of governing autonomous actors that inherit permissions and traverse systems at machine speed, a growing concern for identity governance programs.

news.title: Poland busts SIM-swapping gang tied to millions in crypto theft
news.summary: Polish authorities arrested four members of a cybercrime group that conducted SIM-swapping attacks by breaching telecommunications partners and hijacking email accounts. The gang used these techniques to steal cryptocurrency, with losses totaling millions of dollars.

news.title: Webinar: Why account takeovers remain one of the hardest threats to stop
news.summary: A webinar addresses the ongoing challenge of account takeover (ATO) attacks, which are difficult to detect because attackers operate through legitimate accounts and trusted services. The session focuses on how behavioral AI can be used to identify compromised accounts and automate response workflows.

news.title: Identiverse 2026 Recap: Identity Security for Agentic AI Dominates
news.summary: Identiverse 2026 in Las Vegas focused heavily on identity security for agentic AI as a central theme. The conference highlighted the industry's transition in identity security as organizations manage an expanding universe of AI-driven identity challenges.

news.title: Britive Joins Torq AMP Alliance Program to Accelerate Agentic SecOps
news.summary: Britive has joined the Torq AMP Alliance Program to integrate its runtime Privileged Access Management solution with Torq's agentic AI security operations platform. The partnership involves technical integration, go-to-market collaboration, and joint marketing efforts.

news.title: Virtru and Rocket.Chat Partner to Bring Real-Time Access Governance to Classified Collaboration
news.summary: Virtru and Rocket.Chat have partnered to integrate real-time access governance into classified collaboration channels. The integration automatically removes channel access when user entitlements change, synchronizing channel membership with identity and authorization status.

news.title: Keeper Security Brings Privileged Access Controls to Microsoft Teams With New Keeper Teams App
news.summary: Keeper Security has launched a new Keeper Teams App that embeds privileged access management controls directly into Microsoft Teams. The integration enforces zero standing privilege and provides governed, auditable workflows for credential access within the Teams environment.

news.title: Flare unveils new CTI capabilities and Okta support
news.summary: Flare has announced expanded capabilities for its Flare CTI product and a new Okta integration within its Identity Exposure Management (IEM) offering. The updates extend identity threat intelligence into tactical use cases and agentic workflows.

news.title: Web社内報サービスSOLANOWA、IdP連携の新オプション「ユーザー自動同期（SCIM）」を提供開始
news.summary: Japanese web intranet service SOLANOWA launched a new "User Auto Sync (SCIM)" option for IdP integration. The feature enables automatic user synchronization between identity providers and the SOLANOWA platform via the SCIM protocol.

news.title: Amadey and StealC Malware Network Disrupted, 27M Stolen Credentials Recovered
news.summary: A coordinated law enforcement operation involving Europol and private sector partners including Microsoft disrupted the Amadey and StealC malware network infrastructure. The operation resulted in the recovery of 27 million stolen credentials, which had been harvested by credential-stealing malware used to facilitate ransomware and financial fraud attacks.

news.title: Navigating the hidden costs and governance risks of Microsoft 365 E7
news.summary: Microsoft launched the 365 E7 "Frontier Suite" in May 2026 at $99 per user per month, bundling E5 security capabilities with Microsoft 365 Copilot and the Entra Suite for advanced identity features. The article examines the hidden costs and governance risks associated with this new licensing tier.

news.title: Restrict AWS Management Console access to expected networks with sign-in resource-based policies and RCPs
news.summary: AWS announced support for resource-based policies and resource control policies (RCPs) for AWS Sign-In, enabling network-based access restrictions to the AWS Management Console. The feature allows administrators to restrict console sign-in and AWS CLI login to expected or approved networks using policy controls.

news.title: Show HN: Lelu – gate OpenAI agent actions on confidence and prompt injection
news.summary: An open source project called Lelu has been released on GitHub as an authorization engine for AI agents. It features confidence-aware gating, human-in-the-loop review, policy-as-code controls, and audit trail capabilities for OpenAI agent actions.

news.title: SOFTwarfare Launches on Google Cloud Marketplace, Expands Public Sector Reach With Carahsoft
news.summary: SOFTwarfare has launched its Continuous Identity Defense solution on the Google Cloud Marketplace through a partnership with Carahsoft to expand access to public sector customers. The go-to-market strategy targets both commercial and government customers through Carahsoft's public sector distribution network.

news.title: Securing the service desk: Why social engineering attacks keep succeeding
news.summary: Service desks are being targeted by social engineering attacks aimed at obtaining password resets, MFA changes, and unauthorized access to corporate accounts. Specops Software provides an analysis of how these attacks are executed and outlines defensive measures organizations can implement to protect their service desks.

news.title: Flare Leads on Identity-First Threat Intelligence with Platform Expansions
news.summary: Flare announced new platform expansions focused on identity-first threat intelligence capabilities. The updates aim to help security teams investigate threats and reduce identity risk from a single platform.

news.title: Securing local administrator accounts with Windows LAPS and Microsoft Intune
news.summary: Microsoft's Windows Local Administrator Password Solution (LAPS) is now natively integrated into the Windows operating system, enabling automated rotation of unique local administrator passwords with escrow to a directory service. The article details how administrators can manage LAPS through Microsoft Intune, addressing privileged local account security as part of a PAM-adjacent workflow.

news.title: FortiBleed Attackers Turn Firewalls Into Credential Stealers as Heists Persist
news.summary: Threat actors deployed a Golang-based credential sniffer targeting 430,000 FortiGate firewalls as part of an ongoing global attack campaign. The attackers identified approximately 110 million credentials through the compromised firewall infrastructure.

news.title: FortiBleed Targeted FortiGate Firewalls in 110 Million-Credential Harvesting Operation
news.summary: A large-scale credential-harvesting operation called "FortiBleed," attributed to a Russian-speaking initial access broker, has targeted over 430,000 FortiGate firewalls globally, harvesting 110 million credentials. The campaign, active since February 2026, involves collecting credential lists, brute-forcing accessible systems, and exploiting exposed services on FortiGate devices.

news.title: Trump Order Sets 2030 Deadline for Federal Post-Quantum Crypto Migration
news.summary: President Trump signed Executive Order 14409 on June 22, requiring federal agencies to migrate high-value assets and high-impact systems to post-quantum cryptography by December 31, 2030, with digital signatures deadline set for December 31, 2031. National security systems are placed on a separate migration track under the order.

news.title: Scattered Spider Hackers Plead Guilty on Day 1 of Trial
news.summary: Two members of the Scattered Spider cybercrime group pleaded guilty in the UK to charges related to the August 2024 cyberattack on Transport for London. Scattered Spider is known for sophisticated social engineering and identity-based attacks, including SIM swapping and MFA bypass techniques used to compromise corporate environments.

news.title: Novo Nordisk Breach Highlights Software Development Pipeline Risk
news.summary: A GitHub token leak at Novo Nordisk exposed vulnerabilities in software development pipeline security, specifically around secrets management. The incident illustrates how organizations miscategorize secrets management as a tooling issue rather than an identity and access management problem.

news.title: FIFA Bug Exposes World Cup Streams to Remote Takeover
news.summary: A security vulnerability in FIFA's systems allowed potential remote takeover of World Cup streams due to unenforced Microsoft Entra access controls. The bug demonstrated how misconfigured identity and access management policies could have enabled unauthorized control over broadcast infrastructure.

news.title: Hackers Used Meta’s AI Support Bot to Seize Instagram Accounts
news.summary: Hackers exploited Meta's AI support assistant bot to reset account passwords and seize Instagram accounts, including those of the Obama White House and a U.S. Space Force official. Instructions for manipulating the AI bot to bypass normal authentication and password reset controls were circulated on Telegram.

news.title: Lawmakers Demand Answers as CISA Tries to Contain Data Leak
news.summary: A CISA contractor intentionally published AWS GovCloud keys and other agency secrets to a public GitHub account, prompting congressional inquiries from both chambers. CISA is still working to contain the breach and invalidate the leaked credentials.

news.title: CISA Admin Leaked AWS GovCloud Keys on Github
news.summary: A CISA contractor exposed highly privileged AWS GovCloud account credentials and internal system details in a public GitHub repository until this past weekend. The leaked repository contained files detailing CISA's internal software build, test, and deployment processes alongside the privileged access credentials.
